seoTitle: Santa Teresa vs Shoal Bay — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Santa Teresa and Shoal Bay. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Santa Teresa vs Shoal Bay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Santa Teresa and Shoal Bay.
heading: Santa Teresa vs Shoal Bay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Santa Teresa, Northern Territory and Shoal Bay, Northern Territory. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Santa Teresa has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Shoal Bay (33/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Santa Teresa or Shoal Bay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Santa Teresa has a flood risk score of 35/100, while Shoal Bay scores 22/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Santa Teresa scores 55/100 for bushfire risk. Shoal Bay scores 48/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Santa Teresa or Shoal Bay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Santa Teresa has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.5% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
